mnemonics.title: ABORT Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember: ABORT stands for 'Abnormal Break - Oops! Real Trouble' - it means something went seriously wrong and we need to stop immediately without cleanup.

mnemonics.title: abort vs exit
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'ABORT = A' (Abnormal) vs 'EXIT = E' (Exit normally). Just like in games, 'A' for Abort/Abort mission, 'E' for Exit.

mnemonics.title: No Cleanup Trilogy
mnemonics.content: Remember ABORT doesn't do the 'Three Cs': Call atexit, Clean buffers, Close files.

acronyms.usage: Signal sent when abort() is called; in Unix/Linux, you can catch this signal to handle crashes gracefully.
Functions registered with atexit() are called during normal termination but NOT when abort() is called.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a burning building: abort() is like running out without grabbing your belongings (cleanup). exit() is like carefully locking the door and leaving (proper cleanup).
Draw a flow: abort() → raises SIGABRT → signal handler (if any) → default action: terminate process → optional core dump
Think of buffers as water balloons. exit() carefully puts them down (flushes), abort() just drops everything immediately without putting anything down.

analogies.analogy: Like emergency exit (abort) vs normal exit (exit) in a building. Emergency exit gets you out fast without turning off lights or locking doors. Normal exit takes time but does everything properly.

SIGABRT is like a fire alarm - abort() pulls the alarm, and the default action is to evacuate (terminate). But if someone catches the alarm and handles it, the building doesn't automatically evacuate.
Think of leaving a hotel: exit() is like checking out properly, leaving the room tidy. abort() is like running out in an emergency leaving everything behind.
